name: rm-secure
summary: A secure drop-in replacement for [rm(1)](https://linux.die.net/man/1/rm) with multi-pass data wiping algorithms to prevent data recovery.

documentation.readme: A secure drop-in replacement for rm(1) with multi-pass data wiping algorithms to prevent data recovery.

arguments.name: --continue-on-wipe-error
arguments.description: continue with removal even if wiping fails (default true)

arguments.name: -f
arguments.description: ignore non-existent files and arguments; never prompt prior to removal

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: ignore non-existent files and arguments; never prompt prior to removal

arguments.name: --I
arguments.description: prompt (once) prior to removing more than three files or when removing recursively

arguments.name: -I
arguments.description: prompt (once) prior to removing more than three files or when removing recursively

arguments.name: --one-file-system
arguments.description: when recursing, skip directories that are on a different filesystem

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: remove directories and their contents recursively

arguments.name: --recursive
arguments.description: remove directories and their contents recursively

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

examples: Securely remove (wipe and unlink) the FILE(s).
vsitr     German VSITR standard - 7 passes (BSI/VSITR compliant)
0xAA pattern. Meets German data protection requirements.
dod       US DoD 5220.22-M standard - 3 passes with verification
Meets US Department of Defense data sanitization requirements.
encoding schemes, 4 random lead-out passes. Designed to resist even
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: go install go.dw1.io/rm-secure@latest. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `go install go.dw1.io/rm-secure@latest`. Further invocations from upstream: `rm-secure -h`; `Usage: rm-secure [OPTION]... [FILE]...`. A secure drop-in replacement for rm(1) with multi-pass data wiping algorithms to prevent data recovery.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: A secure drop-in replacement for [rm(1)](https://linux.die.net/man/1/rm) with multi-pass data wiping algorithms to prevent data recovery.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A secure drop-in replacement for rm(1) with multi-pass data wiping algorithms to prevent data recovery.
